content:
​Greetings dear friends,

Delighted to share that my latest article, "𝘛𝘩𝘦 𝘚𝘶𝘪 𝘎𝘦𝘯𝘦𝘳𝘪𝘴 𝘚𝘦𝘱𝘢𝘳𝘢𝘵𝘪𝘰𝘯 𝘰𝘧 𝘗𝘰𝘸𝘦𝘳𝘴 𝘶𝘯𝘥𝘦𝘳 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘐𝘯𝘥𝘪𝘢𝘯 𝘊𝘰𝘯𝘴𝘵𝘪𝘵𝘶𝘵𝘪𝘰𝘯: 𝘑𝘶𝘥𝘪𝘤𝘪𝘢𝘭 𝘙𝘦𝘷𝘪𝘦𝘸 𝘢𝘯𝘥 𝘐𝘯𝘴𝘵𝘪𝘵𝘶𝘵𝘪𝘰𝘯𝘢𝘭 𝘈𝘤𝘤𝘰𝘶𝘯𝘵𝘢𝘣𝘪𝘭𝘪𝘵𝘺” has been published in the commemorative volume, India’s Constitutional Odyssey: 75 Years of Evolution and Global Reach. 

​This volume serves as a scholarly tribute to the resilience and global influence of the Indian constitutional experiment. It brings together a distinguished montage of perspectives from judges, legal scholars, and practitioners from across the Commonwealth and beyond, reflecting on how our constitutional principles have navigated the evolving demands of democracy, governance, and rights protection. 

​In my contribution, I explore the evolving doctrine of separation of powers in India. I argue that our constitutional framework has developed a 𝘴𝘶𝘪 𝘨𝘦𝘯𝘦𝘳𝘪𝘴 approach, one that moves beyond rigid structural classification toward a context-sensitive model. By examining the interplay between judicial review, institutional accountability, and the concept of “𝘫𝘶𝘥𝘪𝘤𝘪𝘢𝘭𝘭𝘺 𝘮𝘢𝘯𝘢𝘨𝘦𝘢𝘣𝘭𝘦 𝘴𝘵𝘢𝘯𝘥𝘢𝘳𝘥𝘴”, the article explains how the judiciary navigates its role in a modern welfare state, particularly when faced with institutional inaction and the imperative of 𝘨𝘰𝘰𝘥 𝘨𝘰𝘷𝘦𝘳𝘯𝘢𝘯𝘤𝘦. 

​It has been a privilege to be a part of this collective intellectual endeavor alongside esteemed jurists and scholars. I look forward to the dialogues this volume will undoubtedly spark among the legal fraternity. Huge shoutout to the Editor-in-Chief Siddharth Sijoria
